# cosmo-smoke — Project Instructions

## Overview

Universal smoke test runner. Standalone Go binary that reads `.smoke.yaml` and runs lightweight smoke tests. Designed for CosmoLabs' ~95-project portfolio.

**Repository**: `github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke`
**Company**: CosmoLabs
**Version**: 0.11.0

## Architecture

```
cmd/
├── root.go          # Cobra root command with banner
├── run.go           # smoke run — main entry point
├── validate.go      # smoke validate — config validation without running
├── init_cmd.go      # smoke init — auto-detect + generate config
└── version.go       # smoke version (ldflags-injected)
internal/
├── schema/          # SmokeConfig structs, YAML parsing, validation
├── baseline/        # Performance baseline storage and comparison
├── runner/          # Assertion engine (29 types), prereq runner, test execution
├── reporter/        # Terminal (Lipgloss) + JSON + Push reporters
├── monorepo/        # Sub-config discovery for monorepo projects
├── dashboard/       # Portfolio dashboard (SQLite storage, API handlers, embedded UI)
└── detector/        # Project type detection + template generation
```

## Key Design Decisions

- **Minimal deps**: Cobra + Lipgloss + yaml.v3 + gjson. No Viper, no Bubbletea.
- **Pure assertions**: All 29 assertion types are pure functions — no side effects.
- **Config inheritance**: `includes:` directive + Go templates (`{{ .Env.FOO }}`).
- **Config-dir-relative**: Commands execute from the config file's directory, not cwd.
- **All errors at once**: Validation returns all errors, not just the first.
- **Reporter interface**: Terminal and JSON reporters are pluggable via interface.
- **Watch mode**: `--watch` keeps smoke resident and re-runs on file changes. fsnotify-backed. 500ms debounce. When OTel is enabled, tracks trace health across runs with a sliding window (last 10 runs). Alerts when health drops below 50%.
- **Retry**: Opt-in `retry: {count, backoff, retry_on_trace_only?}` on test level. Exponential backoff. No side effects on pass-first-try. `retry_on_trace_only` skips retry when the otel_trace assertion confirms the trace was received.
- **Monorepo**: `--monorepo` flag auto-discovers `.smoke.yaml` in subdirectories. Unlimited depth, configurable exclusions.
- **WebSocket**: Stdlib-only WebSocket client. Connect-send-expect pattern with no external deps.
- **gRPC opt-in**: gRPC health check excluded from default build. Use `-tags grpc` to include.

## Build & Test

```bash
go build ./...                    # Build
go test ./...                     # Run all tests (389 total)
smoke run                         # Self-smoke (6 tests)
go build -ldflags "-s -w -X github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/cmd.Version=X.Y.Z" -o smoke .
```

## Commands

```bash
smoke run [--tag X] [--exclude-tag X] [--format terminal,json,junit,tap,prometheus] [--fail-fast] [--timeout 30s] [-f path] [--dry-run] [--watch] [--monorepo] [--otel-collector URL] [--no-otel] [--report-url URL] [--report-api-key KEY] [--baseline] [--baseline-threshold 50]
smoke validate [-f path]
smoke serve [--port 8080] [--dashboard] [--api-key KEY] [--db-path PATH]
smoke init [--force] [--from-running CONTAINER]
smoke version
```

## Assertion Types

| Type | Field | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| exit_code | `*int` | Process exit code match |
| stdout_contains | `string` | Substring match on stdout |
| stdout_matches | `string` | Regex match on stdout |
| stderr_contains | `string` | Substring match on stderr |
| stderr_matches | `string` | Regex match on stderr |
| file_exists | `string` | File exists relative to config dir |
| env_exists | `string` | Environment variable exists |
| port_listening | `{port, protocol?, host?}` | TCP/UDP port is open |
| process_running | `string` | Named process currently running (pgrep -x / tasklist) |
| http | `{url, method?, status_code?, body_contains?, body_matches?, header_contains?}` | HTTP endpoint check |
| json_field | `{path, equals?, contains?, matches?}` | JSONPath assertion on stdout |
| response_time_ms | `*int` | Test duration must not exceed this threshold |
| ssl_cert | `{host, port?, min_days_remaining?, allow_self_signed?}` | TLS cert valid + expiry threshold |
| redis_ping | `{host?, port?, password?}` | Redis PING returns +PONG (RESP protocol) |
| memcached_version | `{host?, port?}` | Memcached `version` command returns VERSION |
| postgres_ping | `{host?, port?}` | Postgres server SSLRequest handshake returns valid protocol byte |
| mysql_ping | `{host?, port?}` | MySQL server sends valid v10 handshake packet on connection |
| grpc_health | `{address, service?, use_tls?, timeout?}` | grpc.health.v1 Health/Check returns SERVING (requires `-tags grpc`) |
| websocket | `{url, send?, expect_contains?, expect_matches?, timeout?}` | WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion |
| docker_container_running | `{name}` | Named Docker container is currently running |
| docker_image_exists | `{image}` | Docker image exists locally |
| url_reachable | `{url, timeout?, status_code?}` | HTTP/HTTPS connectivity check |
| service_reachable | `{url, timeout?}` | External service dependency check |
| s3_bucket | `{bucket, region?, endpoint?}` | S3-compatible bucket accessibility (anonymous HEAD) |
| version_check | `{command, pattern}` | Tool version verification via shell command + regex |
| otel_trace | `{backend?, jaeger_url, service_name?, min_spans?, timeout?, api_key?, dd_app_key?}` | Trace verification with W3C traceparent propagation. Backends: jaeger (default), tempo, honeycomb, datadog |
| credential_check | `{source, name, contains?}` | Credential accessible without leaking value (env\|file\|exec) |
| graphql | `{url, query?, status_code?, expect_types?, expect_contains?, timeout?}` | GraphQL introspection assertion |

Plus `allow_failure: true` on Test for flaky/allowed-failure tests.

## OpenTelemetry Integration

```yaml
otel:
  enabled: true
  jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"
  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"
  trace_propagation: true
```

When enabled, W3C `traceparent` headers are auto-injected into HTTP, gRPC, and WebSocket assertions. The `otel_trace` assertion verifies traces arrived at a collector (supports Jaeger, Tempo, Honeycomb, Datadog backends).

Smoke test results are also exported as OTLP telemetry when `export_url` is configured or `jaeger_url` is set (auto-appends `/v1/traces`). Each test becomes a span with attributes for pass/fail status, duration, and assertion details.

## Output Formats

`smoke run --format X` supports: `terminal` (default), `json`, `junit`, `tap`, `prometheus`. Comma-separated for multiple: `--format terminal,json`. First format goes to stdout, rest to auto-named files (`smoke-results.json`, `smoke-junit.xml`, `smoke-metrics.prom`, `smoke-tap.txt`).

## Detected Project Types

Go, Node (bun/npm), Python, Docker, Rust — each with tailored smoke test templates.

- **All errors at once**: Validation returns all errors, not just the first.
- **Reporter interface**: Terminal and JSON reporters are pluggable via interface.
- **Watch mode**: `--watch` keeps smoke resident and re-runs on file changes. fsnotify-backed. 500ms debounce. When OTel is enabled, tracks trace health across runs with a sliding window (last 10 runs). Alerts when health drops below 50%.
- **Retry**: Opt-in `retry: {count, backoff, retry_on_trace_only?}` on test level. Exponential backoff. No side effects on pass-first-try. `retry_on_trace_only` skips retry when the otel_trace assertion confirms the trace was received.
- **Monorepo**: `--monorepo` flag auto-discovers `.smoke.yaml` in subdirectories. Unlimited depth, configurable exclusions.
- **WebSocket**: Stdlib-only WebSocket client. Connect-send-expect pattern with no external deps.
- **gRPC opt-in**: gRPC health check excluded from default build. Use `-tags grpc` to include.

## Build & Test

```bash
go build ./...                    # Build
go test ./...                     # Run all tests (389 total)
smoke run                         # Self-smoke (6 tests)
go build -ldflags "-s -w -X github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/cmd.Version=X.Y.Z" -o smoke .
```

## Commands

```bash
smoke run [--tag X] [--exclude-tag X] [--format terminal,json,junit,tap,prometheus] [--fail-fast] [--timeout 30s] [-f path] [--dry-run] [--watch] [--monorepo] [--otel-collector URL] [--no-otel] [--report-url URL] [--report-api-key KEY] [--baseline] [--baseline-threshold 50]
smoke validate [-f path]
smoke schema
smoke serve [--port 8080] [--dashboard] [--api-key KEY] [--db-path PATH]
smoke init [--force] [--from-running CONTAINER]
smoke version
```

## Assertion Types

| Type | Field | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| exit_code | `*int` | Process exit code match |
| stdout_contains | `string` | Substring match on stdout |
| stdout_matches | `string` | Regex match on stdout |
| stderr_contains | `string` | Substring match on stderr |
| stderr_matches | `string` | Regex match on stderr |
| file_exists | `string` | File exists relative to config dir |
| env_exists | `string` | Environment variable exists |
| port_listening | `{port, protocol?, host?}` | TCP/UDP port is open |
| process_running | `string` | Named process currently running (pgrep -x / tasklist) |
| http | `{url, method?, status_code?, body_contains?, body_matches?, header_contains?}` | HTTP endpoint check |
| json_field | `{path, equals?, contains?, matches?}` | JSONPath assertion on stdout |
| response_time_ms | `*int` | Test duration must not exceed this threshold |
| ssl_cert | `{host, port?, min_days_remaining?, allow_self_signed?}` | TLS cert valid + expiry threshold |
| redis_ping | `{host?, port?, password?}` | Redis PING returns +PONG (RESP protocol) |
| memcached_version | `{host?, port?}` | Memcached `version` command returns VERSION |
| postgres_ping | `{host?, port?}` | Postgres server SSLRequest handshake returns valid protocol byte |
| mysql_ping | `{host?, port?}` | MySQL server sends valid v10 handshake packet on connection |
| grpc_health | `{address, service?, use_tls?, timeout?}` | grpc.health.v1 Health/Check returns SERVING (requires `-tags grpc`) |
| websocket | `{url, send?, expect_contains?, expect_matches?, timeout?}` | WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion |
| docker_container_running | `{name}` | Named Docker container is currently running |
| docker_image_exists | `{image}` | Docker image exists locally |
| url_reachable | `{url, timeout?, status_code?}` | HTTP/HTTPS connectivity check |
| service_reachable | `{url, timeout?}` | External service dependency check |
| s3_bucket | `{bucket, region?, endpoint?}` | S3-compatible bucket accessibility (anonymous HEAD) |
| version_check | `{command, pattern}` | Tool version verification via shell command + regex |
| otel_trace | `{backend?, jaeger_url, service_name?, min_spans?, timeout?, api_key?, dd_app_key?}` | Trace verification with W3C traceparent propagation. Backends: jaeger (default), tempo, honeycomb, datadog |
| credential_check | `{source, name, contains?}` | Credential accessible without leaking value (env\|file\|exec) |
| graphql | `{url, query?, status_code?, expect_types?, expect_contains?, timeout?}` | GraphQL introspection assertion |

Plus `allow_failure: true` on Test for flaky/allowed-failure tests.

## OpenTelemetry Integration

```yaml
otel:
  enabled: true
  jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"
  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"
  trace_propagation: true
```

When enabled, W3C `traceparent` headers are auto-injected into HTTP, gRPC, and WebSocket assertions. The `otel_trace` assertion verifies traces arrived at a collector (supports Jaeger, Tempo, Honeycomb, Datadog backends).

Smoke test results are also exported as OTLP telemetry when `export_url` is configured or `jaeger_url` is set (auto-appends `/v1/traces`). Each test becomes a span with attributes for pass/fail status, duration, and assertion details.

## Output Formats

`smoke run --format X` supports: `terminal` (default), `json`, `junit`, `tap`, `prometheus`. Comma-separated for multiple: `--format terminal,json`. First format goes to stdout, rest to auto-named files (`smoke-results.json`, `smoke-junit.xml`, `smoke-metrics.prom`, `smoke-tap.txt`).

## Detected Project Types

Go, Node (bun/npm), Python, Docker, Rust — each with tailored smoke test templates.

# cosmo-smoke — Project Instructions

## Overview

Universal smoke test runner. Standalone Go binary that reads `.smoke.yaml` and runs lightweight smoke tests. Designed for CosmoLabs' ~95-project portfolio.

**Repository**: `github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke`
**Company**: CosmoLabs
**Version**: 0.11.0

## Architecture

```
cmd/
├── root.go          # Cobra root command with banner
├── run.go           # smoke run — main entry point
├── validate.go      # smoke validate — config validation without running
├── init_cmd.go      # smoke init — auto-detect + generate config
└── version.go       # smoke version (ldflags-injected)
internal/
├── schema/          # SmokeConfig structs, YAML parsing, validation
├── baseline/        # Performance baseline storage and comparison
├── runner/          # Assertion engine (29 types), prereq runner, test execution
├── reporter/        # Terminal (Lipgloss) + JSON + Push reporters
├── monorepo/        # Sub-config discovery for monorepo projects
├── dashboard/       # Portfolio dashboard (SQLite storage, API handlers, embedded UI)
└── detector/        # Project type detection + template generation
```

## Key Design Decisions

- **Minimal deps**: Cobra + Lipgloss + yaml.v3 + gjson. No Viper, no Bubbletea.
- **Pure assertions**: All 29 assertion types are pure functions — no side effects.
- **Config inheritance**: `includes:` directive + Go templates (`{{ .Env.FOO }}`).
- **Config-dir-relative**: Commands execute from the config file's directory, not cwd.
- **All errors at once**: Validation returns all errors, not just the first.
- **Reporter interface**: Terminal and JSON reporters are pluggable via interface.
- **Watch mode**: `--watch` keeps smoke resident and re-runs on file changes. fsnotify-backed. 500ms debounce. When OTel is enabled, tracks trace health across runs with a sliding window (last 10 runs). Alerts when health drops below 50%.
- **Retry**: Opt-in `retry: {count, backoff, retry_on_trace_only?}` on test level. Exponential backoff. No side effects on pass-first-try. `retry_on_trace_only` skips retry when the otel_trace assertion confirms the trace was received.
- **Monorepo**: `--monorepo` flag auto-discovers `.smoke.yaml` in subdirectories. Unlimited depth, configurable exclusions.
- **WebSocket**: Stdlib-only WebSocket client. Connect-send-expect pattern with no external deps.
- **gRPC opt-in**: gRPC health check excluded from default build. Use `-tags grpc` to include.

## Build & Test

```bash
go build ./...                    # Build
go test ./...                     # Run all tests (394 total)
smoke run                         # Self-smoke (6 tests)
go build -ldflags "-s -w -X github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/cmd.Version=X.Y.Z" -o smoke .
```

## Commands

```bash
smoke run [--tag X] [--exclude-tag X] [--format terminal,json,junit,tap,prometheus] [--fail-fast] [--timeout 30s] [-f path] [--dry-run] [--watch] [--monorepo] [--otel-collector URL] [--no-otel] [--report-url URL] [--report-api-key KEY] [--baseline] [--baseline-threshold 50]
smoke validate [-f path]
smoke schema
smoke serve [--port 8080] [--dashboard] [--api-key KEY] [--db-path PATH]
smoke init [--force] [--from-running CONTAINER]
smoke version
```

## Assertion Types

| Type | Field | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| exit_code | `*int` | Process exit code match |
| stdout_contains | `string` | Substring match on stdout |
| stdout_matches | `string` | Regex match on stdout |
| stderr_contains | `string` | Substring match on stderr |
| stderr_matches | `string` | Regex match on stderr |
| file_exists | `string` | File exists relative to config dir |
| env_exists | `string` | Environment variable exists |
| port_listening | `{port, protocol?, host?}` | TCP/UDP port is open |
| process_running | `string` | Named process currently running (pgrep -x / tasklist) |
| http | `{url, method?, status_code?, body_contains?, body_matches?, header_contains?}` | HTTP endpoint check |
| json_field | `{path, equals?, contains?, matches?}` | JSONPath assertion on stdout |
| response_time_ms | `*int` | Test duration must not exceed this threshold |

| redis_ping | `{host?, port?, password?}` | Redis PING returns +PONG (RESP protocol) |
| memcached_version | `{host?, port?}` | Memcached `version` command returns VERSION |
| postgres_ping | `{host?, port?}` | Postgres server SSLRequest handshake returns valid protocol byte |
| mysql_ping | `{host?, port?}` | MySQL server sends valid v10 handshake packet on connection |
| grpc_health | `{address, service?, use_tls?, timeout?}` | grpc.health.v1 Health/Check returns SERVING (requires `-tags grpc`) |
| websocket | `{url, send?, expect_contains?, expect_matches?, timeout?}` | WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion |
| docker_container_running | `{name}` | Named Docker container is currently running |
| docker_image_exists | `{image}` | Docker image exists locally |

| credential_check | `{source, name, contains?}` | Credential accessible without leaking value (env\|file\|exec) |
| graphql | `{url, query?, status_code?, expect_types?, expect_contains?, timeout?}` | GraphQL introspection assertion |

Plus `allow_failure: true` on Test for flaky/allowed-failure tests.

## OpenTelemetry Integration

```

Smoke test results are also exported as OTLP telemetry when `export_url` is configured or `jaeger_url` is set (auto-appends `/v1/traces`). Each test becomes a span with attributes for pass/fail status, duration, and assertion details.

## Output Formats

`smoke run --format X` supports: `terminal` (default), `json`, `junit`, `tap`, `prometheus`. Comma-separated for multiple: `--format terminal,json`. First format goes to stdout, rest to auto-named files (`smoke-results.json`, `smoke-junit.xml`, `smoke-metrics.prom`, `smoke-tap.txt`).

content.content: Launching skill: goralph

content.text:
# /goralph — AI Loop Dispatch

## MANDATORY — Read Before ANY Action

**When /goralph is invoked, the mechanical work is done by the `goralph` binary — not by you.**

**You CAN and SHOULD use your intelligence to:**
- Analyze the user's request to craft the best task description
- Strategize: what should the goralph loop focus on? What's the right --max? Which engine?
- Prepare context: write a task-file prompt if the task is complex enough to warrant one
- Review results after the loop completes

**You MUST NOT do the implementation work yourself:**
- Do NOT write code, tests, or files that goralph should write
- Do NOT dispatch parallel agents to do the work — goralph runs its own AI engine
- Do NOT execute the task manually with Glob/Grep/Read/Edit
- Do NOT create task lists for manual implementation — one goralph command replaces all of it

**The line**: thinking and planning = yours. Writing code and iterating = goralph's.

### Common Mistakes (DO NOT DO THESE)
- ❌ Reading source files and writing tests/code yourself
- ❌ Dispatching agents to do the work in parallel
- ❌ Using Glob/Grep to find uncovered functions or bugs
- ❌ Creating task lists for manual implementation
- ❌ Analyzing coverage, architecture, or patterns yourself
- ✅ Running `goralph run --task "..." --headless` in background
- ✅ Running `goralph fix BUG-003 --close` for issue fixes

---

Launch GoRalph loops from Claude Code. The loop runs headless in an isolated worktree while your session continues. When it completes, review and merge.

## Usage

```bash
/goralph "increase test coverage to 98%"          # auto-selects unit-tester persona
/goralph "fix the auth bug in handler.go"          # auto-selects debugger persona
/goralph "refactor the runner into smaller files"  # auto-selects refactorer persona
/goralph "security audit the API endpoints"        # auto-selects security-auditor persona
/goralph --task-file docs/prompts/coverage.md --engine opencode --max 30
/goralph --persona unit-tester --engine claude --model sonnet "add tests"  # explicit persona + model
/goralph fix #42 --close
/goralph fix BUG-003 --dry-run
/goralph status
/goralph history --stats
```

## Step 1: Parse Mode

| Input Pattern | Mode |
|---------------|------|
| `"task string"` or `--task-file path` | **Run** |
| `fix <issue-id> [flags]` | **Fix** |
| `status [id]` | **Status** |
| `history [flags]` | **History** |
| `daemon <start\|stop\|status>` | **Daemon** |
| `init` | **Init** |
| `templates` | **Templates** |

## Step 2: Preflight

Run before **Run** and **Fix** modes. Skip for Status/History/Daemon/Init.

```bash
which goralph         # Binary exists?
goralph preflight     # Project readiness (checks .goralph/, config, engines)
```

- If `goralph` not found: tell user to install and **stop**.
- If preflight fails on missing `.goralph/`: **MUST** run `goralph init --no-tui` before proceeding. Do NOT skip this. Do NOT launch `goralph run` without init — it will silently fail in background.
- If preflight fails on daemon: note it — Run mode works without daemon.
- **CRITICAL**: Always use `--no-tui` for both init and run in Claude Code sessions (no TTY available).
- **CRITICAL**: After background launch, immediately verify the process is alive: `sleep 2 && ps aux | grep goralph | grep -v grep`

## Step 3: Execute

### Mode: Run

Build the command from user input.

### Engine Resolution (do this FIRST)

If user passed `--engine X` explicitly → use X. Otherwise, infer:

```bash
branch=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)
```

| Signal | Engine |
|--------|--------|
| User passed `--engine X` | X (highest priority) |
| `--engine auto` | `ccs dispatch` selects from registry + performance history |
| Branch starts with `glm-` or worktree name contains `glm` | `glm` |
| `.goralph/config.yaml` has `engine:` field | that value |
| None of the above | `claude` (fallback) |

**Principle**: The user chose their session model for a reason (cost, speed, capability). GoRalph should respect that choice by default.

**GOrchestra v2 (`--engine auto`)**: Uses `ccs dispatch` registry-based selection. Default engine remains GLM (subscription = free for long loops). Auto mode reads `config/openrouter-models.json` and dispatch performance history. Use `ccs dispatch stats` to see model performance data.

### Persona Resolution (do this SECOND)

If user passed `--persona X` explicitly → use X. Otherwise, **auto-select from task keywords**:

| Task Keywords | Persona | Why |
|---------------|---------|-----|
| test, coverage, unit test, spec | `unit-tester` | 5-phase systematic coverage improvement |
| fix, bug, issue, error, broken | `debugger` | 5-phase reproduce→isolate→analyze→fix→verify |
| refactor, split, extract, clean | `refactorer` | 4-phase measure→test→refactor→validate |
| security, audit, vulnerability, scan | `security-auditor` | 4-phase attack surface→deps→code→report |
| docs, readme, documentation, api docs | `docs-writer` | Documentation-focused |
| review, code review, quality | `reviewer` | Multi-dimensional code review |
| research, investigate, explore, analyze | `researcher` | 4-phase deep investigation |
| backlog, issues, tackle, sprint | `issue-tackler` | 3-phase triage→fix→verify |

If no keywords match → no persona (General mode). Always tell the user which persona was selected and why.

**Available personas**: `unit-tester`, `security-auditor`, `refactorer`, `debugger`, `reviewer`, `researcher`, `issue-tackler`, `docs-writer`, `doc-architect`, `integrator`

### Plan/Prompt Detection (do this THIRD)

If the user references a plan or prompt file, use `--task-file` instead of `--task`:

| User Says | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| "run the test coverage prompt" | Find matching file in `docs/prompts/` via `ccs prompts` |
| "execute the open source plan" | Find matching file in `docs/planning-mode/` |
| "run ROAD-070" | Use `--plan ROAD-070` to auto-generate from roadmap |
| Bare task description | Use `--task "description"` |

When searching for prompts: `ccs prompts` lists recent prompts. Match by keyword in the title.

### Default Flags

| Flag | Default |
|------|---------|
| `--engine` | resolved above |
| `--persona` | resolved above (omit if General) |
| `--max` | `20` (or persona's max_iterations if set) |
| `--headless` | always set |
| `--log` | `json` |
| `--iteration-timeout` | `10m` (prevents engine hangs) |
| `--force` | set when using `--task` (defensive against prompt.md requirement) |

User-provided flags pass through verbatim and override all defaults.

**For inline tasks** (user provides description, not a file):
```bash
goralph run [path] --task "TASK" --persona PERSONA --engine ENGINE --headless --max N --log json --iteration-timeout 10m --force [additional flags]
```
`--force` is required with `--task` because the binary may expect a prompt.md file that doesn't exist for inline tasks.

**For file-based tasks** (user provides --task-file or plan):
```bash
goralph run [path] --task-file PATH --persona PERSONA --engine ENGINE --headless --max N --log json --iteration-timeout 10m [additional flags]
```

**Launch via Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`.** Do not wait for completion.

Report after launch:

```
GoRalph loop launched:
  Task:     <description or file path>
  Persona:  <persona name> (auto-selected | explicit | General)
  Engine:   <engine>
  Model:    <model or "default">
  Max iter: <n>
  Timeout:  <per-iteration timeout>

Monitor: goralph status
History: goralph history --last
```

### Mode: Fix

```bash
goralph fix ISSUE_ID [--close] [--engine ENGINE] [--dry-run]
```

- `ISSUE_ID`: `#42` (GitHub) or `BUG-003` (local CCS issue)
- `--close`: close issue after successful fix
- `--dry-run`: preview plan without executing
- Pass through any additional flags
- **Auto-persona**: Fix mode always uses `debugger` persona (5-phase methodology) unless user overrides

If `--dry-run`: run normally (not background) and show the plan.
Otherwise: launch with `run_in_background: true`.

Report:

```
GoRalph fix launched:
  Issue:  <id>
  Engine: <engine>
  Close:  <yes/no>

Monitor: goralph status
```

### Mode: Status

```bash
goralph status              # All loops overview
goralph status ralph-001    # Detailed iteration breakdown for specific loop
```

Show the output. If any loop has completed, include merge guidance:

```
Completed loop ready for review:
  git -C <worktree-path> log --oneline master..HEAD
  git -C <worktree-path> diff master..HEAD --stat
  ccs merge <worktree-name>
```

### Mode: History

```bash
goralph history             # Table of all loops
goralph history --last      # Last loop details
goralph history --stats     # Aggregates
goralph history --json      # Machine-readable
```

Pass through the user's flags verbatim.

### Mode: Daemon

```bash
goralph daemon start        # Install launchd service + start (port 4567)
goralph daemon stop         # Stop daemon + all loops
goralph daemon status       # Health check (returns JSON)
```

Run the requested subcommand and report the result.

### Mode: Init

```bash
goralph init
```

Report what was created (`.goralph/` directory and config).

### Mode: Templates

```bash
goralph templates
```

Show the template gallery to the user.

### Verification — Prove You Used GoRalph

After launching, you MUST report the loop confirmation from goralph's output. If you cannot show a loop ID or goralph output, you bypassed the skill. Go back and run the goralph command.

---

4. `ccs merge <worktree-name>` — merge to master
5. `ccs kill <worktree-name>` — cleanup (only after successful merge)

## Rules

- **Zero Disruption** — background execution, no new tabs, no focus stealing
- **GoRalph Does Everything** — iteration, testing, safety, logging are GoRalph's job
- **Smart Defaults, User Overrides** — auto-select persona/engine/timeout, but user flags always win
- **Never** use `ccs spawn` (disrupts session), manage worktrees directly, or parse GoRalph internals
- **Engines**: claude, opencode, cursor, codex, qwen, droid
- **Personas**: unit-tester, security-auditor, refactorer, debugger, reviewer, researcher, issue-tackler, docs-writer, doc-architect, integrator
- **Config**: `~/.goralph/config.yaml` (global), `<project>/.goralph/config.yaml` (local)
- **Worktrees**: auto-created at `../ProjectName-worktrees/ralph-{NNN}-{slug}`

# cosmo-smoke - Unit Test Coverage Plan

> Generated by Go Ralph! | Last Updated: 2026-04-20

## Status Legend
- [ ] Pending
- [~] In Progress
- [x] Done
- [!] Blocked

---

## Phase 1: Low-Coverage Packages (baseline, monorepo, cmd)

- [ ] **1.1**: internal/baseline — Add tests for: concurrent file access, very large baseline files (>1000 entries), negative/zero duration values, missing directory for Save, corrupt JSON in Load
- [ ] **1.2**: internal/monorepo — Add tests for: nested subdirectories (3+ levels), .smoke.yaml in hidden dirs (should be excluded), symlinks, empty directories, monorepo with no configs, duplicate project names
- [ ] **1.3**: cmd/validate — Add tests for: config with all 28 assertion types valid, config with OTel enabled and valid, config with includes directive, config with retry policy, config with skip_if conditions, config with env-specific overrides
- [ ] **1.4**: cmd/schema — Add tests for: JSON output roundtrip, schema contains all expected assertion type names, each assertion type has at least one required field, field types are non-empty strings

## Phase 2: Reporter Edge Cases

- [ ] **2.1**: internal/reporter/prometheus — Add tests for: all tests passing (verify metric format), all tests failing, mixed pass/fail/skip, zero duration tests, very long test names with special characters
- [ ] **2.2**: internal/reporter/tap — Add tests for: TAP plan line format, passing tests output "ok", failing tests output "not ok", skipped tests with SKIP directive, multiple tests ordering
- [ ] **2.3**: internal/reporter/push — Add tests for: successful POST to endpoint, endpoint returns non-200, timeout handling, empty API key, empty URL, malformed URL
- [ ] **2.4**: internal/reporter/chain — Add tests for: chaining 3+ formats, chaining with prometheus output to file, verify all formats receive same events via MultiReporter

## Phase 3: Dashboard Coverage

- [ ] **3.1**: internal/dashboard — Add tests for: API handler routes (GET /api/results, POST /api/results), SQLite store creation, result insertion and retrieval, result filtering by project, health check endpoint
- [ ] **3.2**: internal/dashboard — Add tests for: concurrent writes, result with empty project name, result with special characters in test names, pagination behavior

## Phase 4: Runner Assertion Gaps

- [ ] **4.1**: internal/runner — Add tests for: shouldSkip with FileMissing (absolute vs relative paths), filterTests with multiple include tags, filterTests with exclude only, runTest with cleanup command, runTest with dry-run mode, runTest with allow_failure flag
- [ ] **4.2**: internal/runner — Add tests for: parallel execution correctness (verify all tests run), fail-fast stops after first failure, retry with exponential backoff timing, retry_on_trace_only with passing trace assertion

## Phase 5: Schema Validation Edge Cases

- [ ] **5.1**: internal/schema — Add tests for: Validate with websocket valid config, Validate with graphql valid config, Validate with credential_check all three sources, Validate with s3_bucket with endpoint, Parse with Go template environment variables

---

## Rules for Each Iteration

1. Pick the NEXT pending task (lowest numbered unfinished)
2. Write test functions in the appropriate `*_test.go` file
3. Run `go test <package> -v` to verify new tests pass
4. Run `go test ./...` to verify no regressions
5. Commit with message: `test(<package>): <description of tests added>`
6. Mark the task [x] Done in this plan

## Progress Log

| Date | Task | Status | Notes |
|------|------|--------|-------|
| 2026-04-20 | Plan Created | Done | 15 test coverage tasks across 8 packages |

---

## Notes

- Current test count: 394 (378 passed, 2 failed integration, 2 skipped)
- Target: 450+ passing tests
- Do NOT modify production code — only add/modify test files
- All assertions must be pure testable functions — no external dependencies
- Skip tests that need `//go:build integration` tag

## Your Identity

content.content:
through the selected AI engine, commits changes, and signals AGENT_DONE
when complete. Use --task or --task-file to provide a specific task
description instead of relying on .goralph/prompt.md.
--
  - At least one AI engine binary in PATH

Examples:
--
  goralph run --engine opencode --max 10    Use OpenCode, limit to 10 iterations
  goralph run --persona unit-tester         Run as unit-tester persona
  goralph run --worktree my-feature         Run in isolated worktree
--
      --auto-approve                 enable engine's auto-approve/skip-permissions mode
      --auto-review                  Run auto-review classifier after loop completes
      --bail-on-failure int          stop the loop after N consecutive test failures (0 = never)
--
  -e, --engine string                AI engine to use (claude|opencode|cursor|codex|qwen|droid|openrouter)
      --force                        skip the pre-flight readiness check
      --headless                     run without TUI, output to stdout/stderr (for CI/CD)
--
      --iteration-timeout duration   max time per iteration (default 10m); kills hung engines
      --log string                   log format for headless mode (text|json|quiet) (default "text")
      --loop-id string               pre-assigned loop ID, used internally by the daemon spawner
--
      --model string                 model override for the selected engine (e.g., opus, sonnet, gpt-4)
      --no-worktree                  skip auto-worktree creation, run in-place (for quick local experiments)
      --persona string               persona to use (unit-tester|security-auditor|refactorer|debugger|reviewer)
--
      --skip-preflight               skip the engine health check before starting the loop
      --task string                  inline task description (written to .goralph/task.md)
      --task-file string             read task description from this file (overrides --task)

**What worked:**

- 4 commits, 2070 lines of new tests
- All tests that compiled pass (337 from the affected packages)

**What didn't:**
- Loop died after iteration 4 — stopped before tasks 5-15

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- less-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

content.content: Launching skill: continuation-prompt

content.text:
# Continuation Prompt - Session Handoff

Generate a continuation prompt so the next session picks up exactly where this one left off.

## Usage

```bash
/continuation-prompt                              # Auto-analyze session, save prompt
/continuation-prompt "also cover auth refactor"   # Add extra context
/continuation-prompt --quick                      # Display only, no file
/continuation-prompt --status                     # Check pending prompts
```

## Zero-Arg Default Behavior

When invoked without arguments, **do not ask what to include**. You already have full session context. Automatically:

1. **Scan session state**: TaskList, completed work, pending tasks, git log since session start, files modified, decisions made
2. **Decide create vs update**: If a pending prompt from TODAY exists for this branch → update it. Otherwise → create new.
3. **Write the prompt** with everything needed to continue — no user input required.

The user running `/continuation-prompt` with no args means: *"Package up everything I didn't finish so I can resume later."*

## When to Use

| Situation | Command |
|-----------|---------|
| Quick handoff, paste now | `/handoff` |
| Documented handoff, save for later | `/continuation-prompt` (this) |
| End of session (runs this automatically) | `/session-end` |

## Execution

### 1. Check Flags
- `--status`, `--check` → status mode (run `ccs session-status --prompts`, display verbatim)
- `--quick`, `--nofile` → display mode (no file saved)
- `--force` → skip existing prompt check
- Otherwise → file mode (save to docs/prompts/)

### 2. Analyze Current Session (automatic — no user input needed)

**Think holistically, not mechanically.** The continuation prompt is a narrative briefing for your future self — not a checkbox dump. It should convey:

- **Where we stand**: Project momentum, what phase we're in, what's working well
- **What got done this session**: The story of the session, not just a git log
- **Where we're headed next**: Strategic direction, not just pending tasks
- **What didn't get finished and why**: Context matters — was it deprioritized, blocked, or just ran out of time?

Extract from your conversation context:

| Source | What to Extract |
|--------|----------------|
| **Session narrative** | The arc of the session — what we set out to do, what happened, where we ended up |
| **TaskList** | Pending/in-progress tasks = next session's goals |
| **Completed tasks** | Summary of what got done (context for next session) |
| **Git log** | `git log --oneline` since session start — what was committed |
| **Uncommitted changes** | `git status --short` — anything in-progress |
| **Continuation prompts loaded** | If `/run-continuation` was used, reference the source prompt and note which goals were completed vs carried forward |
| **Decisions made** | Key choices that affect future work |
| **Blocked items** | Anything that couldn't be completed and why |
| **Project trajectory** | Roadmap items in flight, feedback patterns, what the next logical milestone is |

### 3. Check for Existing Prompts (File Mode Only)

Skip if `--force`.

**Step 3a — Check pending prompts**:
1. Run `ccs prompts pending --recent 14 --brief`
2. If a pending prompt from TODAY on THIS branch exists → **update it** (add new goals, mark completed ones)
3. If pending prompts exist but for different scope → create new, reference them as carry-overs
4. If no pending prompts → create new

**Only ask the user if genuinely ambiguous** (e.g., two pending prompts both partially relevant). For the common case — just act.

**Step 3b — Carry-Over Detection**:
1. Run `ccs prompts pending --recent 30 --brief`
2. Filter for incomplete goals (`goals_completed < goals_total`)
3. Exclude ABANDONED prompts
4. Reference (don't duplicate) in carry-over section

### 4. Generate Prompt

**The Context section sets the vibe. The GLM Dispatch Rules section prevents queue-bypass mistakes. The Goals section drives the work. The "Where We're Headed" section provides strategic gravity.**

Derive goals from:
1. **Pending TaskList items** → direct conversion to goals
2. **Loaded continuation prompt goals** that weren't completed → carry forward
3. **User-provided context** (if any args were passed) → additional goals
4. **Observed unfinished patterns** → things you started but didn't complete
5. **Strategic next steps** → what logically follows from this session's work (even if not explicitly tasked)

**MANDATORY: Carry-Over Tasks.** Before generating goals, check for incomplete tasks in the current session's TaskList. Any tasks with status `in_progress` or `pending` MUST be included in the prompt as a dedicated `## Carry-Over Tasks` section (see template below). Format each as:
```
- [ ] {task description} (was: {status})
```
This ensures no in-flight work is lost between sessions. If session-end Phase 2.5 flagged incomplete tasks, use that list directly.

Each goal should be specific and actionable — not vague. Include file paths, issue IDs, and concrete next steps.

**MANDATORY**: Always include a `## File Scope` section (see template below) listing `files_modified` and `files_created`. Populate from `git diff master...HEAD --name-only`, planning docs, and goal-referenced files. This is required for `/orchestra dispatch` conflict graph generation.

**Context section guidance**: Write it like you're briefing someone returning after a weekend. They need to feel the project's pulse — not just read a changelog. What's the energy? What's working? What's the next big unlock?

### 5. Output

**File mode** (default):
- Path: `ccs prompts path --slug {slug}` (auto-detects worktree)
- Save prompt with YAML frontmatter
- Validate: `ccs -q prompts check {path}`
- Display: `✅ Continuation prompt saved → {path}`

**Display mode** (`--quick`):
- Output full prompt to terminal
- Display: "Copy and paste into a new session"

## Prompt Scaffolding — Delegate to CCS

**Do NOT hand-roll frontmatter.** Use `ccs prompts init` which emits correct `requires_reading:`, `schema_version: 1`, and three-tier refs automatically:

```bash
ccs prompts init docs/prompts/YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md \
  --title "descriptive title" \
  --branch {branch} \
  --from-plan docs/planning-mode/YYYY-MM-DD-topic.md \
  --from-brainstorm docs/brainstorming/YYYY-MM-DD-topic.md
```

If no plan/brainstorm exists, omit those flags — the command still emits valid schema_version 1 frontmatter. After init, write the body content (goals, context, carry-over) into the file.

## GLM Dispatch Rules

Add to prompts with agent dispatch:

1. **ALWAYS** use `ccs glm-agent exec` for GLM agents (routes through queue with retry)
2. **NEVER** use Agent tool with `model:sonnet`/`model:haiku` for GLM work (bypasses queue, 429 = instant death)
3. Agent tool with `model:opus` is fine for Opus subagents
4. For parallel work: use `/glm-sprint` or `ccs glm-agent exec-batch`

## Prompt Body Template

```markdown
# {Title} - Continuation Prompt

## File Scope
```yaml
files_modified:
  - path/to/modified/file1.go
  - path/to/modified/file2.go
files_created:
  - path/to/new/file1.go
```

**MANDATORY for all continuation prompts.** Populate from:
1. Planning doc's implementation steps (if linked)
2. Git diff of current session's changes: `git diff master...HEAD --name-only`
3. Files explicitly mentioned in goals

This section enables `/orchestra dispatch` to build conflict graphs for parallel worktree management without parsing free-form text.

## Context

{The narrative. Not just "project X on branch Y" — convey the feel:
Where are we in the project's lifecycle? What phase? What's the momentum like?
What did this session accomplish and how does it move the needle?
2-4 sentences that orient the next session immediately.}

## GLM Dispatch Rules

When goals involve dispatching subagents:

1. **ALWAYS** use `ccs glm-agent exec` for GLM agents (routes through queue with retry logic)
2. **NEVER** use Agent tool with `model:sonnet` or `model:haiku` for GLM work (bypasses queue, risks 429 rate limits)
3. Agent tool with `model:opus` is fine for Opus subagents
4. For parallel work: use `/glm-sprint` or `ccs glm-agent exec-batch`

## What Got Done

{Session accomplishments as a cohesive story. Group related work.
Mention commits, issue IDs, and impact — not just file paths.}

## Goals

### [ ] 1. {Goal title}
{Specific instructions: file paths, issue IDs, what to do, acceptance criteria.}

### [ ] 2. {Goal title}
{Same level of detail.}

## Carry-Over Tasks
{Include if the ending session had incomplete TaskList items.
 These are tasks that were in_progress or pending when the session ended.}
- [ ] {task description} (was: in_progress)
- [ ] {task description} (was: pending)

## Carry-Overs
{Only if pending prompts with incomplete goals exist. Reference, don't duplicate:}
1. **[PRIORITY] prompt-title** (X/Y goals)
   → `docs/prompts/YYYY-MM-DD-prompt.md`

## Where We're Headed

{Strategic context: what milestone is next, what roadmap items are in play,
what patterns are emerging from feedback. Help the next session understand
not just WHAT to do but WHY it matters and what comes after.}

## Priority Order
1. {Most important / blocking other work}
2. {High value / quick wins}
3. {Nice to have / exploratory}
```

After creating, validate: `ccs -q prompts check {filename}`

## Related

`/run-continuation` • `/handoff` • `/session-end`

# cosmo-smoke v0.11.x — Test Coverage & Merge Continuation

**Date**: 2026-04-20
**Branch**: master
**Status**: pending

## File Scope

```yaml
files_modified:
  - cmd/run.go
  - internal/reporter/junit.go
  - internal/reporter/junit_test.go
  - CLAUDE.md
  - docs/roadmap/index.yaml
files_created:
  - cmd/validate.go
  - cmd/validate_test.go
  - cmd/schema.go
  - internal/baseline/baseline.go
  - internal/baseline/baseline_test.go
  - internal/schema/export.go
  - internal/schema/export_test.go
files_in_worktree:
  - internal/dashboard/handler_test.go
  - internal/mcp/helpers_test.go
  - internal/runner/runner_extra_test.go
  - internal/schema/schema_extra_test.go
```

## Context

cosmo-smoke is at v0.11.0 with 44 roadmap items all completed, 394+ tests passing. The project is feature-complete. This session added `smoke validate`, `smoke schema`, performance baselines (`--baseline`), and JUnit XML CI metadata. A goralph GLM loop produced 2070 lines of test coverage (4 commits in worktree `ralph-002-increase-unit-test-coverage`) but died after 4 of 15 planned tasks. The remaining work is: merge the goralph output, commit the session's new features, and finish the test coverage plan.

## What Got Done

- **BUG-001 closed**: Watch mode reporter state accumulation — fix was already in commit `b224f52`
- **ROAD-040** (`smoke validate`): Standalone config validation command, 4 tests
- **ROAD-041** (Performance baselines): `--baseline` and `--baseline-threshold` flags, 7 tests, saves/compares timing data
- **ROAD-042** (CI/CD template): Already existed — verified `smoke.yml` reusable + `ci.yml`
- **ROAD-043** (`smoke schema`): JSON export of 28 assertion types, 3 tests
- **ROAD-044** (JUnit XML metadata): Added timestamp, hostname, properties to JUnit output, 2 new tests
- **FB-584 sent**: Edit tool tab-matching feedback to ClaudeCodeSetup
- **Goralph loop**: 4/15 tasks completed in worktree, 2070 lines of tests for dashboard, schema, MCP, runner

## Goals

### [ ] 1. Commit session work to master
Stage and commit all new features from this session: `smoke validate`, `smoke schema`, `--baseline`/`--baseline-threshold`, JUnit metadata, CLAUDE.md updates, roadmap items, BUG-001 closure. Use `/commit-all`.

### [ ] 2. Merge goralph test coverage worktree
The worktree at `ralph-002-increase-unit-test-coverage` has 4 commits with 2070 lines of new tests:
- `256e993` test(dashboard): handler and static file tests (372 lines)
- `5df2b97` test(schema): validation/parse coverage (743 lines)
- `0be42c2` test(mcp): helper function tests (515 lines)
- `65dd4d2` test(runner): monorepo, port, process, assertion tests (406 lines)

Run quality gate: `ccs verify-worktree ralph-002 --fix --approve && ccs merge ralph-002`

### [ ] 3. Relaunch goralph for remaining 11 test coverage tasks
The plan at `.goralph/plan.md` has 11 unfinished tasks (tasks 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 3.2, 4.1, 4.2, 5.1). Relaunch:
```
goralph run --task "Continue .goralph/plan.md test coverage. Pick NEXT unfinished task. Tests only, no production code changes." --persona unit-tester --engine glm --headless --max 15 --log json --iteration-timeout 10m --force --skip-preflight
```
Note: GLM engine may die mid-loop. If so, just relaunch — it picks up from plan.md.

### [ ] 4. Update CLAUDE.md test count after merges
After merging all test coverage, run `go test ./...` and update the test count in CLAUDE.md.

## Carry-Over Tasks

- Uncommitted features: validate, schema, baseline, junit metadata (all implemented, not committed)
- Goralph worktree ralph-002: 4 commits, needs merge
- Goralph plan: 11/15 tasks remaining

## Where We're Headed

Project is at feature-completion (44 roadmap items done). The strategic direction is test coverage hardening before a future v1.0 release (user explicitly said not yet). After test coverage hits 450+, remaining elective directions are: mobile deep link assertions (IDEA-MO1FC22M), and any new ideas from portfolio usage.

## Priority Order

1. Commit session work to master (uncommitted features)
2. Merge goralph worktree (2070 lines of tests waiting)
3. Relaunch goralph for remaining coverage tasks
4. Update docs after all merges

## Technical Context

- **394+ tests** passing on master (378 passed + 2 integration + 2 skipped in clean env)
- **Build**: `go build ./...` — clean
- **Binary**: `go build -ldflags "-s -w -X github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/cmd.Version=0.11.0" -o smoke .`
- **Worktree path**: `/Users/gab/PROJECTS/cosmo-smoke-worktrees/ralph-002-increase-unit-test-coverage/`
- **Disk space issue**: Worktree tests hit "no space left on device" during linking — environmental, not code
- **Goralph config**: `.goralph/config.yaml` has `claude_cmd: glm` — GLM is the correct engine
- **No v1.0 release** — user explicitly said not doing that yet

content.text:
✅ **Continuation prompt saved** → `docs/prompts/2026-04-20-v0.11-test-coverage-continuation.md`

Next session: run `/run-continuation` to pick up. The 4 goals are: commit session work, merge goralph worktree, relaunch goralph for remaining 11 tasks, update docs.

# Session End

Orchestrates end-of-session tasks by delegating to the session-end SOP.

## Usage

```bash
/session-end                    # Interactive (auto-detects trivial/repeat)
/session-end --quick            # Trivial changes: commit only, no docs
/session-end --commit-only      # Just commit, no summary
/session-end --continue         # Continue work: asks lean vs full if prompt exists
/session-end --lean             # Explicit lean: commit + prompt status only
/session-end --complete         # Feature complete, ready to merge
/session-end --release          # Complete + trigger release (master only)
/session-end --skip-summary     # Skip session summary generation
/session-end --status           # Check if session-end was run (no side effects)
/session-end --amend            # Force amend mode (update existing artifacts)
/session-end --amend --release  # Amend + re-bump version
/session-end --force            # Bypass amend detection, run fresh
/session-end patch              # Session-end + patch release (bugfixes, settings)
/session-end minor              # Session-end + minor release (new features)
/session-end major              # Session-end + major release (breaking changes)
```

Natural language also works: "just commit", "continue tomorrow", "feature complete", "with release", "lean mode", "patch up version bump", "minor release"

## Step 1: Parse Mode

| Input | Mode |
|-------|------|
| (no args) | full |
| `--quick`, "just commit", "trivial" | quick |
| `--commit-only` | commit-only |
| `--continue`, "continue tomorrow" | continue |
| `--lean`, "lean mode" | lean |
| `--complete`, "feature complete" | complete |
| `--release`, "with release" | release |
| `--skip-summary` | skip-summary |
| `--status` | status |
| `--amend`, "amend mode" | amend |
| `--amend --release` | amend-release |
| `--force` | force (bypass amend detection) |
| `patch` | patch |
| `minor`, "minor release" | minor |
| `major`, "major release" | major |

## Step 2: Status Check

If mode=status: run `ccs session-status`, display output, **EXIT**.

## Step 2.5: Amend Detection

Check if session-end was already run this session:
```bash
ccs session-status --json
```

If the JSON shows session summary phase with status "✅" (already completed):
1. Print: `⚠️ Session-end already ran. Running in **amend mode** — will update existing artifacts.`
2. Find existing artifacts from today:
   ```bash
   summary=$(ls docs/sessions/Session-*$(date +%Y-%m-%d)*.md 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
   release_notes=$(ls docs/release-notes/*$(date +%Y-%m-%d)*.md 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
   continuation=$(ls docs/prompts/*$(date +%Y-%m-%d)*continuation*.md 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
   ```
3. Pass artifact paths to Phase 3 agents (amend-aware prompts below)

If `--force` flag → skip amend detection, run fresh.

**Amend-aware agent prompts:**

Session summary agent (amend mode):
```
An existing session summary was written earlier this session: {summary_path}
Read it. Append a "## Continued Work" section with new commits and accomplishments.
Don't rewrite from scratch — add to the existing document.
New commits since last session-end: {git log --oneline since last commit}
```

Continuation prompt agent (amend mode):
```
An existing continuation prompt was written earlier: {continuation_path}
Read it. Mark completed goals as [x]. Add new goals from continued work.
Update "What Got Done" section. Don't create a new file — update the existing one.
```

**Amend mode Phase 4**: Skip release unless `--amend --release` is explicitly passed.

## Step 3: Worktree Redirect

```bash
git rev-parse --git-common-dir  # If != ".git", it's a worktree
```

If worktree detected:
- quick/commit-only → handle inline (just /commit-all)
- continue/lean → invoke `/worktree-end --continue` via Skill tool
- complete → invoke `/worktree-end --complete` via Skill tool
- All others → invoke `/worktree-end` via Skill tool

Report: `🌿 Worktree detected — redirecting to /worktree-end [mode]`

**Do NOT continue after redirect.**

## Step 4: Create Phase Checklist (MANDATORY)

**Before executing ANY phase**, create tasks for every active phase. This gives the user real-time visibility and prevents phase skipping.

Use the mode-phase matrix to determine which phases are active:

**Full/complete/release mode tasks:**
```
TaskCreate: "Phase 0+1: Pre-commit hygiene (ccs session-end-pre)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.5: Code simplification (/simplify)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.75: Verification gate (superpowers:verification-before-completion)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.8: Code review gate (superpowers:code-reviewer)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.9: Integration check (optional, manifest-gated)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 2: Commit changes (/commit-all)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3: Parallel blast (post-commit)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3c: Idea capture (observations from session)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 4: Release decision (uses blast output)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 6: Finalize (ccs session-end-finalize)"
```

**Quick/commit-only mode tasks:**
```
TaskCreate: "Phase 2: Commit changes (/commit-all)"
```

**Lean mode tasks:**
```
TaskCreate: "Phase 2: Commit changes (/commit-all)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 6.1: Update prompt status"
```

**Continue mode tasks:**
```
TaskCreate: "Phase 0+1: Pre-commit hygiene"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.75: Verification gate (superpowers:verification-before-completion)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.8: Code review gate (superpowers:code-reviewer)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.9: Integration check (optional, manifest-gated)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 2: Commit changes (/commit-all)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3: Parallel blast (post-commit)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3c: Idea capture (observations from session)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3d: Tool reflection (/reflect)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 4: Release decision"
TaskCreate: "Phase 6: Finalize (ccs session-end-finalize)"
```

## Step 5: Execute Phases

**For each phase task, you MUST:**
1. Mark the task `in_progress`
2. Announce: `**Phase N: [name]**`
3. Execute the phase
4. Show the result to the user
5. Mark the task `completed`

If a phase is not applicable (e.g., no code files changed → skip simplify), announce it:
`**Phase 1.5: Code simplification** — skipped (no code files changed)`
and mark the task completed with a note.

### Sequential Phases (before commit)

| Phase | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **0+1** | `ccs session-end-pre` → read JSON → report findings. Extract `change_profile` from JSON for phase gating. Also run `ccs workcheck` to verify goal completion and feed the continuation prompt with gap analysis. |
| **1.5** | Invoke `/simplify` via Skill tool. **Gate:** Skip if `change_profile.category` is `docs-only`, `config-only`, or `test-only`. Only run on `source`/`mixed`. |
| **1.75** | **Verification gate** — conditional on `change_profile.category`: skip if `docs-only` or `config-only` ("no source changes"); skip if `test-only` ("already verified"); if `source`/`mixed` with `has_untested_source=true`, run scoped tests on `go_packages_touched` only (not `./...`); otherwise skip ("all changes already tested"). |
| **1.8** | **Code review gate**: If `change_profile.category` is `source` or `mixed`, dispatch `superpowers:code-reviewer` agent on `git diff HEAD`. Review high-confidence findings (>80). Fix critical issues before committing. Skip if `docs-only`, `config-only`, or `test-only`. |
| **1.9** | **Integration check (optional)**: If `integration-manifest.yaml` exists and session touched files in 2+ platform directories (check git log), offer: `🔍 Multi-platform session detected — run integration check?` with options "Run quick check (`ccs audit integration --quick`)" / "Run docs check (`ccs audit integration --docs`)" / "Skip". Use AskUserQuestion. Skip silently if no manifest exists. |
| **2** | Invoke `/commit-all` via Skill tool |

### Phase 2.25: Conditional Rebuild

Based on `change_profile` from Phase 0+1:

```
IF change_profile.cli_changed == true:
  → `ccs rebuild --only ccs`

ELIF changed files touch tools/openrouter/:
  → `ccs rebuild --only openrouter`

ELIF changed files touch tools/cosmohooks/:
  → `ccs rebuild --only cosmohooks`

ELSE:
  → skip: "Rebuild — skipped (no binary-affecting changes)"
```

### Phase 2.75: Auto-Stage Changelog

Scan commits since last tag for changelog-worthy entries:
```bash
git log --oneline $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)..HEAD --format="%s"
```

For each commit matching conventional format (`--type` is **required**):
- `feat(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type added`
- `fix(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type fixed`
- `refactor(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type changed`
- `perf(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type changed`
- `security(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type security`
- `deprecate(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type deprecated`
- Breaking changes: add `--breaking` flag
- Skip: `chore`, `docs`, `test`, `ci`, `style`

**CCS changelog add usage:** `ccs changelog add "description" --type {added|fixed|changed|deprecated|removed|security} [--breaking]`

**Dedupe semantics (FB-485):** Always scan every commit since the last tag and call `ccs changelog add` for each. Let the tool dedupe internally — do NOT pre-check with `ccs changelog status`. `AddEntry` in `internal/changelog/staging.go` enforces two dedup rules:

| Rule | Matches on | Effect |
|------|-----------|--------|
| Source-based | Non-"manual" source (e.g. `FEAT-034`, `BUG-176`) matching an existing entry's Source | Returns error `entry with source X already exists` |
| Description-based | Case-insensitive trimmed description matching an existing entry | Returns error `duplicate entry already exists` |

Treat both errors as **expected non-failures**. Count them as "skipped (already staged)" and continue scanning. Only log unexpected errors (I/O, malformed YAML).

Display:
```
Auto-staged N changelog entries:
  ✨ added: X
  🐛 fixed: Y
  (A skipped: already staged | B skipped: chore/docs/test)
```

If no feat/fix/refactor commits found, skip silently. If the scan runs on a session where entries were manually staged beforehand, the two sets merge cleanly via the dedupe rules above.

### Phase 2.5: Task List Preservation (before Phase 3)

If tasks exist in the current session's TaskList:

1. Read all tasks (completed, in_progress, pending, failed)
2. Build a task summary table:
   ```
   | Status | Count |
   |--------|-------|
   | Completed | N |
   | In Progress | N |
   | Pending | N |
   | Failed | N |
   ```
3. Include this table in the session summary data (passed to Phase 3 agents)
4. If any tasks are **incomplete** (in_progress or pending), flag them for the continuation prompt:
   - Store the list of incomplete task descriptions for `/continuation-prompt` to pick up
   - These become carry-over tasks in the next session's prompt

**Skip if no tasks exist.** This phase is a data collection step — it does not modify tasks.

### Phase 3a: CCS Commands (instant)

Run all CCS commands in parallel via Bash:
```bash
ccs session-end-post --json     # Post-audit findings
ccs doc-audit --json            # Documentation gaps
ccs roadmap reconcile --json    # Superseded roadmap items
ccs verify review --brief       # SVQ status
ccs memwatch report             # Memory usage report (Memwatchtower)
```

### Phase 3b: Sonnet Agents (background)

Dispatch in a SINGLE message with `run_in_background: true`:
- Agent A: Invoke `/session-summary` (or amend existing summary if amend mode)
- Agent B: Invoke `/continuation-prompt` (or update existing prompt if amend mode)
- Agent C: Doc-gap fixer (Sonnet) — see below. **Skip if no new/modified `cmd/*.go` files this session.**

**Agent C prompt template:**
```
You are filling documentation gaps for CCS commands modified this session.

1. Run `ccs doc-audit --json` to get all gaps
2. Run `git diff --name-only HEAD~N..HEAD` (N = session commit count) to get modified files
3. Filter doc-audit gaps to ONLY commands whose source file (tools/ccsession/cmd/<name>.go) was modified this session
4. For each filtered gap with "README" in missing_from:
   - Read the command source for usage/flags
   - Run `ccs <name> --help` for description
   - Generate `READMEs/commands/README.<name>.md` following the pattern of existing READMEs (see READMEs/commands/README.audit.md as template)
5. Stage the new files (do NOT commit — main thread handles commits)
6. Report: "Generated N READMEs for: <list>"

Constraints: Only generate for commands MODIFIED this session. Do NOT touch the other 100+ undocumented commands — that is a separate refactor.
```

**Do NOT wait for Phase 3b to proceed to Phase 4.** You'll be notified when agents complete.

### Phase 4: Release Decision (after CCS only)

**Do NOT wait for Phase 3b agents.** CCS output from 3a is all that's needed.

**This phase is a DETERMINISTIC SCRIPT, not a judgment call.**

```
1. Run `ccs version --release-check --json`
2. Parse JSON result

3. IF hold_active AND hold condition matches work done this session:
   → Run `ccs version --unblock "<condition text>"`
   → Run `ccs version --unhold`
   → Re-run `ccs version --release-check --json`

4. IF mode=release/patch/minor/major:
   → type = that explicit type (override suggested_type)
   ELSE:
   → type = suggested_type from JSON

5. IF all_conditions_met=true AND type is set AND hold_active=false:
   → EXECUTE `ccs version --bump <type> --highlights "..."`
   → This is LOCAL and REVERSIBLE. There is NO reason to defer.
   → You are NOT asking permission. The SOP authorizes this action.

6. IF NOT all_conditions_met:
   → Show why and move on

7. IF mode=amend AND --release NOT passed:
   → Skip release entirely
```

**POST-PHASE ASSERTION:** If release-check showed `all_conditions_met=true` but no version bump was executed, you have violated this SOP. Go back and execute step 5 NOW, or state a concrete technical blocker (not "should we wait" — a real blocker like failing tests or missing changelog).

Version bumps are local commits. They are reversible via `git reset`. "Deferring to next session" is NOT acceptable when conditions are met — context will be lost and the user will have to re-engage.

### Phase 3b.complete: Agent Output

When Sonnet agents complete (you'll be notified), commit their output. If amend mode, verify amendments look correct (appended, not duplicated).

**While agents run**, collect CCS output and review:
- Roadmap items in-progress — assess if any should be updated
- Ideas, lessons, feedback — file if warranted

**Present consolidated dashboard when all complete:**
```
Phase 3: Parallel Blast
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✅ Post-audit: N findings           [CCS — Phase 3a]
✅ Doc gaps: N undocumented         [CCS — Phase 3a]
✅ Roadmap: N items reconciled      [CCS — Phase 3a]
✅ SVQ: N pending items             [CCS — Phase 3a]
✅ Session summary                  [Agent A — Phase 3b]
✅ Continuation prompt              [Agent B — Phase 3b]
✅ Doc-gap READMEs (N generated)    [Agent C — Phase 3b, skipped if no cmd changes]
```

### Phase 3c: Idea Capture (mandatory in full/complete/release modes)

Before finalizing, reflect on ALL observations from the session — code review findings, simplify suggestions, patterns noticed, problems spotted, and ideas generated during implementation.

**Steps:**
1. Collect observations from:
   - Code review (Phase 1.8) low-priority findings that weren't fixed
   - Simplify (Phase 1.5) suggestions that were deferred
   - Implementation work — patterns, problems, or improvements noticed
   - Post-audit (Phase 3a) findings worth tracking
   - Any "we should..." or "it would be nice if..." moments from the session

2. Present to user as a categorized list:
   ```
   💡 Session observations:
   
   IDEAS:
   - [description] → would file as: ccs idea add "title"
   
   BUGS:
   - [description] → would file as: /bug "title"
   
   IMPROVEMENTS:
   - [description] → would file as: ccs feedback send . "title"
   ```

3. For each item the user approves (or all if user says "file all"):
   - **Ideas**: `ccs idea add "title" --body "full context from session"`
   - **Bugs**: Use `/bug` skill with `--description`, `--severity`, `--fix-direction`, `--affected-files`
   - **Improvements**: `ccs feedback send . "title" --body="context"`
   - Include enough session context that a future session can act on it without re-discovery

4. If no observations worth filing → announce: `**Phase 3c: Idea Capture** — skipped (no actionable observations)`

**Gate:** Skip in quick, commit-only, and lean modes. Active in full, complete, release, and continue modes.

### Phase 3d: Tool Reflection

```
Phase 3d: Tool Reflection
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Review tools, SOPs, and skills used this session.
Any gaps, friction, or problems worth feeding back to CCS?
```

Invoke `/reflect` via Skill tool. It will self-review tool usage and auto-send any actionable feedback.

**Gate:** Skip in quick, lean, and commit-only modes. Active in full, complete, release, and continue modes.

### Phase 6: Finalize

| Phase | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **6** | `ccs session-end-finalize` |

## Step 6: Mark Status Line (MANDATORY)

**After SOP completes (regardless of mode), ALWAYS run:**
```bash
cosmohooks mark-session-end
```

This flips `◻ /session-end` → `✅ /session-end` in the status line. The SOP also calls this, but this step is a failsafe — run it even if you think it already ran. Idempotent.

## Related

`/commit-all` - Multi-commit workflow
`/continuation-prompt` - Handoff for next session
`/release` - Version management
`/worktree-end` - Worktree session lifecycle
`/efficiency-check` - Full efficiency analysis

content.text:
# Commit-All v2 — CCS-Delegated Intelligent Commit Workflow

CCS handles mechanical scanning and grouping. Claude handles creative message writing and review decisions.

## Usage

```bash
/commit-all                    # Analyze and commit all changes
/commit-all --session          # Only changes from this session
/commit-all master --push      # Commit, merge to master, push
/commit-all --skip-review      # Skip code review for trivial changes
/commit-all --skip-quality     # Skip build/lint/test checks
```

## Execution

### Phase 0: WORKCHECK (optional, 2 seconds)

If an active continuation prompt exists, run `ccs workcheck` to surface any unverified goals or documentation gaps before committing. Display the output to the user but do NOT block on it — proceed to Phase 1 regardless.

Skip if: `--skip-quality` flag is set, or no active prompt exists (`ccs workcheck` returns error).

### Phase 1: ANALYZE (CCS — fast, zero tokens)

Run `ccs commit-analyze --json` (or `--json --session` if `--session` flag).

This returns structured JSON with:
- File changes grouped by semantic proximity
- Quality gate results (build/lint/test)
- Diff summaries per group (regex-extracted function/type/test names)
- Issue references (auto-detected from `docs/issues/` affected_files)
- `review_recommended: true` if total diff > 100 lines

If `--skip-quality` was passed, add `--skip-quality` flag.

### Phase 2: REVIEW + REGROUP (Claude — creative)

Review CCS's suggested groups. For each group, decide:
- Accept the grouping, or reorganize (split/merge) based on session context
- Split groups that mix `feat` and `fix` changes
- Merge groups that are logically the same feature

If `review_recommended` is true and `--skip-review` not set: invoke `superpowers:requesting-code-review` for architectural/security review.

If 3+ groups, create a TaskCreate per group for progress visibility.

### Phase 3: CRAFT MESSAGES (Claude — the main value)

**Principle: Commit messages are permanent history. You're not losing history — you're building it.**

Write commit messages for each group following conventional commit standards. Scale detail to significance:

| Type | Subject | Body | Example Body |
|------|---------|------|-------------|
| **feat** | What it enables | Structured bullets: what each component adds, design decisions, test counts | `- Add TokenBucket with per-key limits and automatic cleanup` / `- Design: sliding window over fixed counters for accuracy vs memory` / `- 21 tests covering burst, expiry, and concurrent access` |
| **fix** | What was broken | Root cause + how it's fixed | `Root cause: race condition in session cleanup — goroutine reads map while reaper deletes.` / `Fix: copy keys slice before iteration, delete under write lock.` |
| **test** | What's covered | Test count + edge cases + coverage delta (if known) | `- 14 tests covering: valid input, boundary values, nil handling, concurrent writes` / `- Coverage: internal/auth 72% → 88% (+16pp)` / `- Regression test for BUG-045 (duplicate session IDs)` |
| **refactor** | What improved | Before/after reasoning | `Before: 340-line monolith handling parse + validate + emit.` / `After: three focused functions, each independently testable.` |
| **perf** | What's optimized + metric | Before/after numbers | `Reduce CLAUDE.md from 461 to 350 lines (~24% reduction, ~660 tokens saved)` |
| **security** | What's hardened | Vulnerability + fix | `Root cause: unsanitized input interpolated into sh -c. Fix: validate via allowlist before interpolation.` |
| **audit** | What was assessed + score | Agent count, key scores, critical findings, files produced | `Dispatch 8 parallel Opus agents for comprehensive codebase analysis:` / `- Code Quality: 70/100, Core Logic: 60/100` / `- 29 audit files, 6 bugs filed, 12 roadmap items` |
| **docs** | What's documented | Concise, 1-line body max | `Update API reference for v2 auth endpoints.` |
| **ci** | What pipeline changed | Brief explanation | `Add ast-grep scan step to pre-commit checks.` |
| **build** | What build changed | Brief explanation | `Update Makefile port assignments for monorepo services.` |
| **chore** | What changed | No body needed for metadata | *(none)* |

#### Format Rules

**Subject**: `type(scope): imperative summary` — max 72 chars, lowercase after colon.
**Body**: Blank line after subject. Imperative bullet points: "Add X", "Fix Y", "Remove Z" — never paragraphs.
**Footer**: `Refs: FEAT-xxx` when CCS detected issue refs. `BREAKING CHANGE:` when applicable.

#### Mandatory Rules

- **Never** produce a single-line message for `feat` commits touching 3+ files — the body is required.
- **Always** include the WHY for features: what does this enable? What problem does it solve?
- **Always** include test counts when tests are added: "21 tests covering..." not just "add tests".
- **Always** reference issues/roadmap items from CCS analysis in footer: `Refs: FEAT-xxx, ROAD-yyy`.
- **No AI attribution** — no co-authored-by, no emoji signatures, no "Generated with" lines.
- **No prose paragraphs** — bullets only. Each bullet starts with an imperative verb.

#### Test Commit Quality Gate (FEAT-356)

For every `test(scope):` commit the body MUST contain all three:

1. **Test count** — exact number added/modified. `- Add 14 tests` not `- Add tests`.
2. **Edge cases listed** — at least one bullet naming specific scenarios covered (boundary, nil, concurrency, error paths, regression targets).
3. **Coverage delta** — when `go test -coverprofile` or equivalent was run, include the before→after percentage for the tested package. Omit the bullet entirely when coverage wasn't measured; never fabricate numbers.

If any of the three is missing, surface it before building the commit plan and ask the user: "Test commit for {scope} is missing {count|edges|coverage}. Add it or confirm omit?" Never silently ship a thin `test(...)` commit.

#### Examples

**Bad — too thin** (no body for a multi-file feature):
```
feat(crypto): add crypto module
```

**Bad — over-engineered** (prose wall, repeats the diff):
```
feat(crypto): add crypto module

This commit introduces a comprehensive cryptographic module that provides
encryption and decryption capabilities. The module was designed with careful
consideration of security best practices and performance requirements. We
evaluated several approaches including AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305, and
XSalsa20 before settling on AES-256-GCM as the primary cipher suite due
to hardware acceleration support on modern processors...
(200 more words)
```

**Good — structured, scaled to significance**:
```
feat(crypto): add AES-256-GCM encryption module

- Add Encrypt/Decrypt with authenticated data support
- Add key derivation from passphrase via Argon2id
- Design: AES-GCM over ChaCha20 for hardware acceleration on target infra
- 18 tests covering: roundtrip, bad key, tampered ciphertext, empty input

Refs: FEAT-091
```

**Good — fix with root cause**:
```
fix(reaper): prevent panic on concurrent session cleanup

Root cause: goroutine iterates session map while reaper deletes entries.
Fix: snapshot keys under read lock, delete individually under write lock.

Refs: BUG-072
```

**Good — chore, no body needed**:
```
chore: update Go dependencies to latest patch versions
```

### Integration Check (Advisory)

If an `integration-manifest.yaml` exists in the project root or `docs/`, and the staged changes span 2+ platform directories (check with `git diff --cached --name-only` against the manifest's platform paths):

```
⚠️ Changes span {platform1} + {platform2} — consider: ccs audit integration --quick
```

This is advisory only — display the suggestion, do not auto-run. Skip if no manifest exists.

### Phase 4: EXECUTE (CCS — fast)

Build JSON plan and pipe to `ccs commit-batch`:
```json
[
  {"files": ["path/file1.go", "path/file2.go"], "message": "feat(scope): subject\n\nBody"},
  {"files": ["path/file3_test.go"], "message": "test(scope): subject"}
]
```

Run: `echo '<plan>' | ccs commit-batch --json`

Or with `--dry-run` first to preview.

### Phase 5: POST (optional)

- If target branch specified: `ccs merge <target>`
- If `--push`: `ccs sync --push`

## Output

```
✅ Created N semantic commits
   1. abc1234 feat(auth): implement JWT authentication
   2. def5678 docs(auth): document auth endpoints
   3. ghi9012 test(auth): add auth coverage

[If merged:] ✅ Merged to: master
[If pushed:] ✅ Pushed to remote
```

## Note on Versioning

Version bumps are NOT automatic. Use `/release` for proper releases.

## Related

`/release` • `/pit-stop` • `/merge` • `ccs commit-analyze` • `ccs commit-batch`
