content.text:
8 groups. Let me regroup these into logical commits:

1. **feat(baseline): performance baseline tracking** — group 6 (ROAD-041)
2. **feat(cmd): smoke validate and schema commands** — groups 5 + 8 (ROAD-040, ROAD-043)
3. **feat(reporter): JUnit XML CI metadata** — group 7 (ROAD-044)
4. **docs: roadmap items, BUG-001, CLAUDE.md updates** — group 4
5. **chore: goralph config, session metadata** — groups 1, 2, 3

input.command:
echo '[
  {"files": ["internal/baseline/baseline.go", "internal/baseline/baseline_test.go", "cmd/run.go"], "message": "feat(baseline): add performance baseline tracking

- Add --baseline flag to save and compare test timings across runs
- Add --baseline-threshold flag (default 50%) for regression sensitivity
- Store per-test durations in .smoke-baseline.json (JSON, config-dir-relative)
- Flag regressions when current duration exceeds baseline by threshold%
- 7 tests covering: save/load, compare with regressions, new tests, zero baseline, update merge

Refs: ROAD-041"},
  {"files": ["cmd/validate.go", "cmd/validate_test.go"], "message": "feat(cmd): add smoke validate command

- Standalone config validation without running tests
- Reports all errors at once via schema.Validate
- 4 tests covering: valid config, invalid config, missing file, multi-error output

Refs: ROAD-040"},
  {"files": ["cmd/schema.go", "internal/schema/export.go", "internal/schema/export_test.go"], "message": "feat(schema): add smoke schema command for JSON export

- Export all 28 assertion types with fields, types, and required flags as JSON
- Useful for editor integrations, auto-completion, and tooling
- 3 tests covering: type count, required fields, valid JSON roundtrip

Refs: ROAD-043"},
  {"files": ["internal/reporter/junit.go", "internal/reporter/junit_test.go"], "message": "feat(reporter): add timestamp, hostname, and properties to JUnit XML

- Add ISO 8601 timestamp on testsuite element
- Add hostname attribute for CI identification
- Add properties element with project, passed, failed, skipped counts
- 2 tests covering: timestamp/hostname presence, property values

Refs: ROAD-044"},
  {"files": ["CLAUDE.md", "docs/roadmap/index.yaml", "docs/roadmap/items/ROAD-040.yaml", "docs/roadmap/items/ROAD-041.yaml", "docs/roadmap/items/ROAD-042.yaml", "docs/roadmap/items/ROAD-043.yaml", "docs/roadmap/items/ROAD-044.yaml", "docs/issues.yaml", "docs/issues/BUG-001.yaml", "docs/ideas/2026-04-19-watch-mode-reporter-state-reset.md"], "message": "docs: add ROAD-040..044 items, close BUG-001, update CLAUDE.md

- ROAD-040: smoke validate command (completed)
- ROAD-041: performance baseline tracking (completed)
- ROAD-042: CI/CD GitHub Actions template (already existed)
- ROAD-043: smoke schema JSON export (completed)
- ROAD-044: JUnit XML CI metadata (completed)
- BUG-001: watch mode reporter state reset (closed, fix in b224f52)
- CLAUDE.md: update version to 0.11.0, test count, new commands"},
  {"files": [".goralph/plan.md", ".goralph/prompt.md", ".goralph/state.yaml", ".goralph/task.md", ".gorchestra/fingerprint-cache.json", "GOrchestra/intel/architecture.json", "GOrchestra/intel/status.json", "docs/conversation-transcripts/2026-04-19_170647_e7a99f76.md", "docs/conversation-transcripts/2026-04-19_192235_ace50f4f.md", "docs/sessions/transcripts/e7a99f76_auto_2026-04-19_e7a99f76-4edf-451d-814a-268dc99f1c29.jsonl", "docs/prompts/2026-04-20-v0.11-test-coverage-continuation.md", "docs/feedback/outgoing/2026-04-19-claudecodesetup-edit-tool-fails-to-match-tab-indented-go.md"], "message": "chore: session metadata, goralph config, continuation prompt"}
]' | ccs commit-batch --json 2>&1

content.text:
# Run Continuation - Resume Previous Work

Load a continuation prompt to pick up where a previous session left off.
**Delegates to `ccs prompts load-context` for three-tier enforcement (ADR-005).**

## Usage

```bash
/run-continuation              # Show latest, confirm before loading
/run-continuation --yes        # Auto-load most recent (skip confirm)
/run-continuation --list       # Show all prompts, pick by number
/run-continuation --main       # Force main repo prompts (when in worktree)
/run-continuation api-feature  # Fuzzy match filename
/run-continuation upgrade <name>       # Upgrade old-schema prompt, then load
/run-continuation upgrade <name> --run # Upgrade + immediately execute
```

## Invariants — Non-Negotiable

These cannot be skipped. Session 294 shipped P-05 while bypassing them and regressed the exact discipline the system enforces.

| # | Invariant | Why |
|---|-----------|-----|
| I1 | `ccs prompts load-context <path>` is THE loader. Never fall back to raw `Read` / `ccs read` on the prompt file as a workaround. | The command errors on missing `requires_reading:` refs. Raw Read silently swallows the failure mode this entire subsystem exists to prevent. |
| I2 | Every `### [ ] G-NN` or `### [ ] N.` goal in `## Goals` gets ONE `TaskCreate` call BEFORE any code runs. | Without per-goal tasks, goals get silently dropped. S294 skipped this and shipped 1/19 goals while reporting "done." |
| I3 | Every file in `requires_reading:` gets read in full before writing ANY code. | The plan/brainstorm IS the spec. Reading only the prompt = implementing from partial context. |
| I4 | When a commit covers a `P-*` / `BR-*` deliverable, append its SHA to the prompt's `implemented_commits:` via Edit. | Otherwise `ccs prompts verify` cannot attribute coverage. Silent drift. |
| I5 | Before session-end, run `ccs prompts verify <prompt> --mechanical-only` and confirm new CONFIRMED_COVERED verdicts for this session's work. | The only machine-checkable answer to "did we actually ship what the prompt said?" |

## Execution

### 1. Resolve Path

| Flag | CCS Command | Result |
|------|-------------|--------|
| (default) | `ccs prompts --latest` | Path to most recent prompt |
| `--list` | `ccs prompts --limit 10` | Numbered picker via AskUserQuestion |
| `--main` | `ccs prompts --main --latest` | Force main repo (in worktree) |
| fuzzy arg | `ccs prompts` + match | Resolve filename |

Default flow: show filename via AskUserQuestion ("Load this?" / "Show all"). `--yes` skips confirm.

### 2. Load Context — Mandatory Gate (I1)

```bash
ccs prompts load-context <prompt-path> --no-bodies
```

**If exit ≠ 0, STOP.** Surface the error; await guidance. Do NOT switch to `Read` as a workaround — the exit is the feature.

Exit contract:
- `0` — requires_reading manifest validated; proceed
- `1` — a required file is missing (fix the prompt or the missing file)
- `3` — a path escapes the project root (security gate; always fatal)

`--no-bodies` emits the manifest only. Full bodies come in step 3.

If the prompt is old-schema (no `requires_reading:` in frontmatter, just prose refs in the body):

1. Run `ccs prompts upgrade <prompt-path> --apply` — promotes prose refs to frontmatter, injects the required-reading section, sets schema_version: 1
2. Loop back to step 2 (load-context) with the upgraded prompt

Alternatively, use `/run-continuation upgrade <name>` which combines both steps. Add `--run` to auto-execute after upgrade.

### 3. Read Every Required File (I3)

For each entry in the manifest:
- ≤200 lines → `Read` direct
- \>200 lines → `ccs read <path> --full`

Do not skim. If the plan is 300 lines, read 300 lines.

### 4. Create Tasks Per Goal (I2)

Parse the prompt's `## Goals` section. For every `### [ ] G-NN Title` or `### [ ] N. Title`, emit ONE `TaskCreate`:

- `subject`: the goal's title (with G-NN prefix)
- `description`: first paragraph under the heading (~200 chars)
- `activeForm`: present-continuous form

Also TaskCreate every `## Carry-Over Tasks` item. Non-negotiable.

### 5. Execute

With tasks + context loaded, follow the prompt body. `TaskUpdate → in_progress` when starting each goal; `completed` when done.

### 6. Mid-Session Discipline (I4)

After each commit covering a `P-*` / `BR-*` deliverable:

1. `git log -1 --format=%H` → capture SHA
2. Edit the prompt's `implemented_commits:` list, appending the SHA with a `# P-NN` / `# BR-NN` comment
3. If the commit closes a goal: `ccs prompts set-goals <prompt> <done> <total>`

*Known gap: no `ccs prompts add-commit` CLI yet — Edit is the path. File a FEAT if this friction recurs.*

### 7. Session-End Gate (I5)

Before `/session-end` or `/commit-all`:

```bash
ccs prompts verify <prompt-path> --mechanical-only
```

Confirm coverage moved up for the deliverables this session was meant to ship. If it didn't — the work isn't done, regardless of what TaskList shows.

## Related

`/continuation-prompt` • `/handoff` • `/session-end` • `ccs prompts --help` • `docs/architecture/ADR-005-three-tier-documentation-system.md`

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- less-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
